title: Oxypetalum oblongifolium
taxonomy: Oxypetalum oblongifolium belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, comprising over 4,000 species. Oxypetalum oblongifolium is a genus of flowering plants native to Brazil, and is often classified as a type of flowering shrub or vine. Within the Apocynaceae family, Oxypetalum oblongifolium is part of the tribe Plumerieae. The exact classification of Oxypetalum oblongifolium may also be specified at the subfamily level, specifically as part of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae.

intro: Oxypetalum oblongifolium, also known as the "False Poppies" or "Gilia", is a flowering plant species native to the Americas, characterized by its showy, bell-shaped flowers and narrow, oval-shaped leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
